title: Orchis troodi
taxonomy: Orchis troodi is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Orchis, which falls under the family Orchidaceae. This flowering plant is native to the Eastern Mediterranean region and is known for its showy, fragrant flowers. The Orchis genus is further divided into various subgenera, with Orchis troodi being placed in the subgenus Orchis. The Orchidaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, comprising over 30,000 species worldwide. As a member of this family, Orchis troodi is closely related to other orchids found in the region.

intro: Orchis troodi, also known as the Cypriot Orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, respiratory problems, and as a natural remedy for boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Orchis troodi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Orchis troodi is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction to treat respiratory issues, and it is also used in the production of capsules and tinctures.
